meta_title: Scrybe — Local subtitles for your own broadcasts
meta_description: Scrybe runs whisper.cpp on your machine and pipes live captions straight into OBS as a browser source. Local, open-source, no cloud.
hero_subtitle: Scrybe runs whisper.cpp on your machine and pipes live captions straight into OBS as a browser source. No cloud, no API keys, no per-minute billing.
hero_subtitle_caution: Still in early development — use with caution.
hero_caption_float: and this is what it looks like on stream
feat_local_body: All processing happens on your machine. Your audio never leaves the box — no API keys, no cloud latency, no subscription.
feat_audio_body: Select any audio device. Caption your own voice, game audio, a VoD you're reacting to — even virtual audio cables.
feat_obs_body_post: into OBS and the subtitles appear. Tune align, background and transparency live.
feat_settings_body: Swap models, toggle translate, trim silence, etc. Great out of the box, but allows you to customize your experience.
how_heading: Four steps from install to on-stream.
how_body: Scrybe is a Tauri app — install it like any native binary. First-run grabs your selected model, then it serves the overlay on localhost for OBS to pick up.
how_step_2: Pick an input and a model
whisper_stat_cost_label: no API bills, no account creation
whisper_body_p1_pre: whisper.cpp is Georgi Gerganov's port of OpenAI's Whisper speech-recognition model to plain C/C++. Same models, same weights — just stripped of the Python stack and compiled down to something small enough to ship inside a desktop app.
whisper_body_p2_pre: Scrybe embeds it directly. Audio goes in, text comes out, nothing round-trips to a server. You pick the model size —
whisper_body_p2_post: when you want real accuracy — and tune the flags yourself.
